videos.title: The "Boomer Problem" Is Coming
videos.description: Is it a Tsunami? Nope, but it will impact the market over time.

insights.summary: The 'silver tsunami' of boomer-owned real estate entering the market will be a slow drip over 10-20 years, not a crash, creating a gradual increase in motivated seller inventory. Boomers currently own 41% of all property and average age 72, meaning estate-driven and age-related distressed situations will increase steadily. For wholesalers, this signals a long-term pipeline of aging-in-place sellers who will eventually face pressure to sell due to health, estate, or financial reasons.
key_lessons: Boomers own 41% of all U.S. property and average age 72 — targeting this demographic for cold calls and direct mail is a high-probability long-term strategy
Inventory will increase slowly over 5-20 years, meaning deal flow from aging sellers will grow but won't crater ARVs — good for both acquisitions and disposition pricing
Inherited properties (heirs who receive boomer real estate) are a prime distressed seller segment — heirs often want to liquidate quickly
Home price appreciation is expected to be slow, so locking in assignment fees now before further affordability pressure matters less than volume and consistency
Millennial buyer demand remains strong, supporting end-buyer pools for disposed deals even as inventory rises
trends: Gradual increase in aging-seller and estate-driven inventory over the next 10-20 years creates a growing motivated seller pipeline
Slow home price appreciation and affordability pressure may soften ARVs, requiring tighter acquisition underwriting
Inherited property transactions expected to rise as boomer wealth transfers to millennial heirs who may prefer to liquidate
acquisition_one_liners: I know a lot of homeowners your age are trying to figure out what makes sense for them and their family — have you given any thought to what you want to do with the property long-term?
A lot of people in similar situations tell me they just don't want the hassle of repairs or dealing with the market — is that something you're concerned about?
We work with a lot of families going through estate situations where they inherited a property and just want a simple, fast solution — is that kind of flexibility something that would help you?
I understand you're not in a rush, and that's totally fine — a lot of the folks we work with just want to know their options before they have to make any decisions. Can I share what we could offer?
With the market the way it is, a lot of sellers are finding it harder to time things perfectly — would a guaranteed cash offer with a flexible closing date take some of that pressure off?
disposition_one_liners: Inventory from aging homeowners and estates is only going to increase over the next decade — getting into deals now while competition is still manageable is a smart play.
This is a boomer-owned property — the comps are solid and with millennials still actively buying, your exit as a fix-and-flip or rental is well-supported.
Estate and age-related deals like this one tend to come with deferred maintenance priced in — the spread is there for you to make your numbers work.
tips_and_tricks: Build a dedicated list of homeowners 65+ in Miami-Dade — filter by age and length of ownership (20+ years) for highest equity and motivation probability
Add 'estate' and 'inherited property' as a separate list segment in your CRM; heirs are often out-of-state and highly motivated to close fast
As appreciation slows, tighten your MAO formula — don't rely on future price increases to bail out a weak deal; underwrite conservatively
Use the demographic trend as a conversation opener on cold calls to older sellers — it creates empathy and positions you as informed, not predatory
Track probate filings in Miami-Dade County as a lead source — boomer estate volume will increase and probate leads are high-intent motivated sellers
insights.south_florida_relevance: No direct mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach. However, the boomer ownership demographic is highly relevant to South Florida — Miami-Dade has a significant retiree and aging homeowner population, many with long-held properties at high equity. The estate and inherited property opportunity is particularly strong in coastal South Florida communities with high boomer concentration (e.g., Kendall, Hialeah, Homestead, and parts of Broward).

insights.summary: Rick presents a simple volume-based formula for wholesaling success: 3,000 calls per deal from government lists equals 12 deals per year at 36,000 total calls. The core message is to replace vague income goals with a concrete call volume north star. This reframes the wholesaler's mindset from outcome-focused to activity-focused.
key_lessons: 3,000 calls from government lists = 1 wholesaling deal (use this as your baseline conversion benchmark)
Set 36,000 annual calls as your north star metric instead of a dollar income goal
Government lists (probate, tax delinquent, code violations, etc.) are the recommended lead source for this call volume strategy
Focus on controllable inputs (calls made) rather than uncontrollable outputs (deals closed)
Reverse-engineer your deal goal: 12 deals/year = 36,000 calls/year = 3,000 calls/month = ~143 calls/day
trends: Activity-based KPI tracking replacing revenue-based goals in wholesaling operations
Government list cold calling as a primary distressed seller acquisition channel
High-volume dialing strategies becoming the standard for competitive wholesale markets
acquisition_one_liners: I came across your property through public records and wanted to reach out — are you open to a cash offer?
I know this might be out of the blue, but we work with homeowners in situations like yours and can close fast with no fees.
We're not agents — we buy directly, so there's no commissions, no repairs, no hassle on your end.
I just want to have a quick conversation to see if what we offer makes sense for your situation — no pressure at all.
A lot of the folks we help just want a simple, straightforward exit — is that something you'd be open to talking about?
disposition_one_liners: We have a property under contract in [area] — solid spread, clean title, looking to assign to the right buyer fast.
This one checks the boxes: distressed seller, below-market price, and we can close on your timeline — you interested?
We're running a high-volume acquisition model so deals come through consistently — want to be on our priority buyer list?
tips_and_tricks: Track calls made daily as your #1 KPI — 143 calls/day hits the 36,000/year target across a 5-day work week
Use government lists (tax delinquent, probate, code enforcement, pre-foreclosure) for higher distress-to-contact ratio
Assign call volume targets per rep: with 2 callers, each needs ~1,500 calls/month to hit 12 deals/year at team level
Don't measure success by offers made or deals closed week-to-week — measure by call volume consistency
Build a simple daily call log dashboard so the team can self-monitor progress toward the 36,000 annual target
insights.south_florida_relevance: No specific mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach markets. However, the government list strategy is directly applicable — Miami-Dade has robust public records for tax delinquency, probate, and code violations, making this call volume model executable in your market.

videos.title: How to Make Cash Offers on Vacant Land! #shorts
videos.description: Unlock deals with a simple reverse price anchor. Instead of aiming high, set a lower 'usual' price to influence the buyer's perception and secure better offers. #SalesTactics #Negotiation #DealMaking #BusinessTips

insights.summary: Rick demonstrates a 'reverse price anchor' negotiation tactic for making cash offers on vacant land, where instead of leading with your ideal price, you attribute a lower number to a partner to soften the anchor. The clip cuts off before the full technique is revealed, but the core mechanic is using a third-party (partner) as the reason for the low offer. This is a classic deflection-and-anchor combo useful in any motivated seller conversation.
key_lessons: Use a 'reverse price anchor' by attributing the low offer to a partner rather than yourself — reduces seller resistance and keeps you likable
Silence after dropping the anchor number is a deliberate tactic — let the seller respond without filling the dead air
Framing the offer as the partner's preference ('he usually likes to buy these for around X') creates psychological distance from the lowball
The technique works on vacant land but the psychology is directly transferable to distressed house acquisitions
trends: Vacant land wholesaling is growing as a niche within the broader wholesale space
Short-form content (Shorts) is being used to distribute bite-sized negotiation scripts to wholesalers
acquisition_one_liners: I'm telling my partner here, and he usually likes to buy these lots for around $30,000.
You know, ideally in the right world, I would love to buy this for more, but my partner typically comes in around [X].
My partner is the one putting up the funds, and he usually wants to be around [X] on something like this.
disposition_one_liners: My partner is the one who sets the buy price on these — I'm just putting the deal together for him.
I've got a buyer who typically targets these at around [X] — if we can get close to that, I can move fast.
tips_and_tricks: After dropping the anchor number, go silent — do not negotiate against yourself
Attribute the low number to a third party (partner, buyer, fund) to stay likable and avoid direct confrontation
Practice the exact phrasing until it sounds natural — scripted delivery kills the technique
This works best when the seller has already shown motivation — qualify before anchoring
The reverse anchor sets the floor; be ready to come up slightly to create the illusion of a win for the seller
insights.south_florida_relevance: No direct mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach. However, vacant lots and infill land deals are highly active in Miami-Dade and surrounding areas — this tactic is directly applicable to the team's market when pursuing land acquisitions or sellers with vacant lot inventory.

Get XLeads & Get the Best All in One Wholesaling Software + 25k Free Skiptraces⤵️

Want to Learn wholesaling for free? Join our Free Course & Community ⤵️

Summary ⤵️
This video explains a simple “reverse funnel” strategy to help beginners get their first wholesaling deal in 2026. The creator emphasizes starting with large government lists of motivated sellers, such as probates, pre-foreclosures, tax delinquencies, and water shut-offs. These lists are then filtered and enhanced using tools like skip tracing or AI-driven data to find the most likely sellers. The strategy involves contacting leads through calls, texts, and even leaving notes on properties to maximize response rates. He highlights that success comes from either investing money to save time or putting in more hours to do everything manually. Overall, the system is designed to narrow down thousands of leads into a few high-quality opportunities that can result in profitable deals.

insights.summary: Zachian presents a 'Reverse Funnel System' for getting a first wholesaling deal using layered government lists, AI-driven lead scoring, and multi-touch outreach (call, text, sticky note). The core strategy prioritizes free or low-cost motivated seller data from county government sources over paid marketing lists. Total system cost is approximately $177/month or zero if using free tools, targeting roughly 2,700 leads per month.
key_lessons: Pull 9 government lists (probate, code violations, pre-foreclosure, liens, tax delinquency, evictions, water shut-offs, arrest records, fire damage) from your local county — these are free and represent the most motivated seller data available

Stack three list tiers into one funnel: government lists (~2,000 records) + AI-scored leads 750+ (~500 records) + satellite/drone property condition scoring 70+ (~200 records) = ~2,700 total leads per month

Execute a 3-touch outreach sequence on the full list: call all 2,700 leads, text the ~2,100 who don't answer, and physically leave sticky notes on pre-foreclosure, code violation, and tax delinquency properties
Water shut-off lists are heavily underutilized — owners who can't pay a water bill are in severe financial distress and highly motivated to sell at a discount
Minimum viable budget to run this system is $177/month (skip trace software + dialer + texting); full free route requires 70-80 hours/week using TruePeopleSearch and Google Voice
trends: AI-powered lead scoring (predicting seller motivation from 0-1000 using credit history and behavioral data) is becoming a standard tool replacing broad list pulls
Satellite and drone imagery used to score property condition remotely ('droning for dollars') is emerging as a next-gen driving-for-dollars replacement
Wholesaling competition is increasing but so is deal volume — more data access means operators with better list segmentation are pulling ahead of cold callers working generic lists
acquisition_one_liners: Hey, this is Zach. Got a quick question about your property. Please give me a call back.
I saw your property and I wanted to reach out — I buy houses in any condition for cash and I wanted to see if you'd be open to an offer.
I noticed there may be some challenges with the property and I wanted to reach out directly to see if selling is something you'd consider.
I work with a local team that buys houses as-is, no repairs needed, no commissions — just a straightforward cash offer if the numbers work for you.
I know you may be going through a tough situation with the property — I just want to see if there's anything I can do to help and potentially make you an offer.
disposition_one_liners: I have a distressed property under contract in [area] — it's a code violation and pre-foreclosure, priced to move. Are you actively buying in that zip code right now?
I've got a deal that came off a government list — probate situation, motivated heir, below-market price. Let me send you the details and see if it fits your buy box.
This one came from a water shut-off list so the seller is highly motivated — I can assign this contract quickly if you can close in two weeks.
tips_and_tricks: For sticky notes on doors, keep the message extremely short and curiosity-driven — name, one line about their property, and a callback number only; no mention of buying or offers
Prioritize water shut-off and arrest record lists — they are the least-worked lists in most markets because competitors don't pull them, giving you a cold outreach advantage
When skip tracing government lists manually (free route), use TruePeopleSearch.com — budget 30 hours per 2,000 records and batch the work early in the week before calling begins
Only leave sticky notes on pre-foreclosure, code violation, and tax delinquency properties — probate properties with heirs not on-site make physical outreach less effective and potentially awkward
Use Google Voice as a free texting and calling number when starting out — it keeps your personal number off lists and allows basic lead tracking by conversation thread
insights.south_florida_relevance: No direct mention of Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, or South Florida. However, the government list strategy is highly applicable to South Florida — Miami-Dade County publicly maintains code violation, tax delinquency, pre-foreclosure, eviction, and probate records, many accessible via the county property appraiser and clerk of courts websites. The water shut-off list is particularly actionable in Miami-Dade given WASD (Water and Sewer Department) jurisdiction. Code violations in Miami-Dade are tracked through Miami-Dade County Code Compliance and individual municipal code enforcement offices. These lists are free and largely untapped by competing wholesalers in the market.

insights.summary: 26-year-old Ray Rodriguez runs a 94-deal, $1.5M revenue wholesaling operation in Las Vegas spending only $109K in marketing by exclusively targeting real estate agents — calling MLS listings to build relationships, sending 100 offers/day, and converting agent relationships into off-market pocket listings. Roughly 70 of 94 deals came from agent relationships (off-market), not MLS. He tried and abandoned direct-to-seller marketing (direct mail, PPC, PPL) after a 6-figure loss, concluding agent-based acquisition is far more efficient for his team.
key_lessons: Build a '40-block referral board' — identify 40 people (mostly agents) who will each send you 1 deal/year; at $20K average that's $800K/year in assignment fees from referrals alone

Use MLS offers as relationship-builders, not just deal-getters — 100 offers/day generates agent conversations that convert into future off-market pocket listings, even when the current offer is rejected
Track 'relationships built' as your leading KPI — it is the most predictive metric for future deal flow, more than leads or offers

Double-ending is the agent's incentive: agents bring pocket listings directly to you instead of the MLS so they can keep the full 6% commission rather than splitting — make sure every agent knows this benefit when you pitch them

Direct-to-seller channels (direct mail, PPC, PPL) produced poor ROI compared to agent outreach — $30K direct mail got zero leads; PPC closed deals but with bad margins on low-equity properties; agent channel at fraction of cost produced 94 deals
trends: Agent-to-investor referral pipelines are outperforming direct-to-seller marketing in competitive markets — off-market pocket listings from agents now represent the majority of deals for top operators
Wholesalers are abandoning PPC and direct mail in favor of relationship-based MLS prospecting as lead quality and cost-per-deal deteriorate on paid channels
Private lending within your network (students, event contacts, referrals) is becoming a primary capital-raising strategy for operators who need to flip or hold without institutional debt
acquisition_one_liners: Hey, I have a client that's interested in purchasing this property — let's talk numbers.
What's kind of got your seller considering selling?

This property probably won't work for us right now, but let me tell you exactly what we're looking for — if you ever get something like that, I want to be the first call you make before it ever hits the MLS.

We're not going to be the highest offer today, but we close fast, we're easy to work with, and if you ever have a pocket listing or something you want to move quietly, just send it straight to us and you keep the whole commission.
We made a pretty low offer — low for your standards, but pretty fair for where we see things as investors — is there any flexibility there?
disposition_one_liners: We've got an off-market deal — here's the address, here's the ARV, here's what we're in it for — let me know if your buyer wants to take a look before we send it to the full list.
We close on this regularly with our buyer pool — if you've got capital sitting, I can get you deal flow consistently in this market.
We're looking for buyers who move fast and don't re-trade — if that's you, get on our list and we'll make sure you see everything first.
tips_and_tricks: Set up keyword searches (fire, TLC, investment, as-is) on the MLS as a secondary filter, but know that the real deals come from agents whose listings have NO distress keywords — everyone else is already calling those keyword listings

Use a VA + template system to send 100 offers/day on the realtor contract — only the address, price, parcel number, and agent contact info need to change per offer; cost is ~$600/month for a VA vs $500-600/month for offer software

Structure your team in two tiers: junior callers who prospect listings and qualify motivation, and senior closers who take warm handoffs and negotiate — this scales output without the principals being in every call

Track every stage of your pipeline: addresses to leads → leads to appointments set → appointments attended → offers made → contracts signed → contracts assigned → closed deals — know your conversion rate at every step so you can diagnose where deals are falling out
Build Google reviews aggressively — agents check your reputation before referring off-market deals; a strong review profile removes friction from agents who don't know you personally
insights.south_florida_relevance: No direct mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach. However, the agent-outreach model is highly transferable to South Florida: Miami-Dade has a massive realtor population with high listing volume, many agents double-ending deals, and a competitive MLS environment where pocket listings from agent relationships would give Buying Hero a significant edge over direct mail competitors. The 'no distress keywords' offer strategy is particularly relevant in South Florida where equity-rich listings are common but sellers aren't always advertising motivation.

videos.title: Zoning Laws Just Changed Nationwide And You Missed It
videos.description:
Most people have no idea this is happening, but zoning reform real estate is quietly rolling out across the country.

These zoning changes real estate are allowing what used to be single-family homes to now support multiple units like duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes. This shift in housing density real estate is opening up a massive zoning opportunity real estate investors are missing.

With this kind of real estate development strategy, you can dramatically increase real estate land value and tap into new middle housing strategy trends without going through complicated approvals.

This is one of the biggest real estate market opportunities right now, and the investors who understand it early will have a serious edge.

#realestateinvesting #wholesalerealestate #jamildamji #zoningReformRealEstate #zoningChangesRealEstate #middleHousingStrategy #duplexTriplexFourplexInvesting #realEstateDevelopmentStrategy #zoningOpportunityRealEstate #realEstateInvestingTips #propertyValueIncreaseRealEstate #realEstateMarketOpportunity #realEstateInvestorEducation #housingDensityRealEstate #realEstateLandValue #investorMindsetRealEstate #realEstateOpportunity2026 #youtubeShortsRealEstate

As always, I appreciate each one of you guys for joining me on this YouTube journey.  The goal is to hit 100k subscribers!  You can help me out by liking the video and sharing it with a friend if you found it helpful.

insights.summary: Zoning reform is sweeping the country quietly, converting single-family zoning to allow 1-4 units by-right in major metros across at least 10 states. This creates a massive, underrecognized land value arbitrage opportunity for wholesalers who can identify and pitch affected properties before sellers or competing buyers realize the upside. The play is to lock up single-family distressed deals and market them to builders and developers at a premium based on new density potential.
key_lessons: Single-family lots in rezoned areas now legally permit 1-4 units without a full zoning hearing — this dramatically increases land value overnight
Most sellers and even buyers are unaware of these zoning changes, creating a first-mover information advantage for wholesalers who research local zoning updates
Distressed sellers on newly upzoned lots may be dramatically underpricing their properties — this widens your spread potential significantly beyond the $20K average target
Disposition strategy should shift toward builders, developers, and investors who understand density plays — not just standard buy-and-hold or flip buyers
Proactively audit your existing lead lists and pipeline against local zoning maps — deals you already have under contract or in negotiation may have hidden density value you haven't priced in
trends: Nationwide 'missing middle' housing reform converting SFR zoning to multifamily by-right (1-4 units) across 10+ states
Information asymmetry window is open now — sellers unaware of upzoning = underpriced acquisitions
Developer/builder buyer pool expanding as entitled land becomes more accessible without lengthy rezoning hearings
acquisition_one_liners: Hey, I just want to be upfront with you — I'm not sure if you're aware, but the zoning on properties like yours has actually changed recently, and that affects what I can offer you.
A lot of homeowners in your area don't realize their property now qualifies for more than just a single family home — I work with buyers who specifically look for that.
I want to make sure you get a fair number — the land itself has more potential than most people realize right now, and I'd hate for you to leave money on the table with someone who doesn't know that.
The market is shifting and the value of your lot specifically may be higher than what a traditional buyer would tell you — let me walk you through what I'm seeing.
I specialize in properties that have development potential — yours might qualify, and I'd love to take a look before you make any decisions.
disposition_one_liners: This one is zoned for up to four units by-right — no hearing, no variance, you can move straight to permits. Builders are paying a premium for exactly this.
The zoning reform that just hit this area means this lot has multifamily density without the entitlement risk — that's a game changer for your pro forma.
We're seeing developer buyers pay 20 to 40 percent over traditional ARV on these by-right multifamily lots right now — this deal fits that profile.
tips_and_tricks: Pull the current zoning map for every Miami-Dade and Broward county lead before your next call — identify which ones fall in recently upzoned corridors
Add a zoning check step to your acquisitions workflow: cross-reference each address with the county's GIS or zoning portal before making an offer
Build a separate buyer list of small developers, infill builders, and ADU contractors who specifically want by-right multifamily lots in South Florida
When comp-ing deals in upzoned areas, price the land as a development site, not just using residential ARV — use cost-per-unit or cost-per-buildable-foot metrics
Monitor Florida state legislature and Miami-Dade county zoning bulletins for new middle-housing or ADU reform announcements to stay ahead of the market
insights.south_florida_relevance: HIGH RELEVANCE: Florida is actively engaged in housing density reform. Miami-Dade and the City of Miami have already implemented zoning changes including the Miami 21 code and ongoing ADU/infill updates. The 'Live Local Act' passed in Florida (2023) is a direct local parallel to what this video describes — it preempts local zoning to allow multifamily density near commercial corridors statewide. Your team should be cross-referencing every SFR acquisition lead against Live Local Act eligibility and Miami-Dade's current zoning atlas immediately. This is not hypothetical for your market — it is already law in Florida.

insights.summary: This Jerry Norton (Flipping Mastery) video features live seller calls with his son, demonstrating real-time cold calling and warm lead conversion techniques for wholesaling. No transcript is available, so specific dialogue and tactics cannot be extracted. Based on channel context, the video likely covers objection handling, rapport building, and offer presentation with motivated sellers.
key_lessons: No transcript available — full lesson extraction is not possible; review video directly for live call scripting examples
Jerry's free cold calling scripts are available at flippingmastery.com/ccs — actionable resource for the acquisitions team
Warm Leads Daily (WarmLeadsDaily.com) is promoted — may be worth evaluating as a lead source supplement to cold calling
Free Motivated Seller Scripts available at FreeSellerScripts.com — direct resource for the team's call reps
Free Instant Offer Calculator (FreeOfferCalculator.com) and Deal Analyzer (MyDealAnalyzer.com) are free tools that could streamline offer delivery on calls
trends: Warm/pre-qualified lead delivery services are being marketed as alternatives or supplements to cold list calling
Educational content pairing live calls with coaching (parent-child format) signals a push toward accessible, real-time wholesaling training
Heavy tooling ecosystem (comps, offer calculators, KPI trackers) suggests the industry is moving toward systematized, software-assisted acquisitions
acquisition_one_liners: No verbatim seller phrases could be extracted — transcript was unavailable for this video
Refer to Jerry's free script pack at FreeSellerScripts.com for word-for-word motivated seller language
Refer to flippingmastery.com/ccs for the 5-Step Warm Call Process scripts directly applicable to your team's calls
disposition_one_liners: No verbatim buyer/JV phrases could be extracted — transcript was unavailable for this video
Refer to flippingmastery.com/cbsyt for Jerry's free Cash Buyer Scripts
Refer to flippingmastery.com/cwyt for the free Wholesale JV Contract template for JV partner conversations
tips_and_tricks: Pull Jerry's FREE Cold Calling Scripts (flippingmastery.com/ccs) — 5-step warm call process is directly usable by your acquisitions manager
Use FreeOfferCalculator.com during live calls to generate quick offers — reduces hesitation and speeds up the offer conversation
CompMyDeal.com is a free comp tool that can support ARV calculations in real time before presenting offers
100preforeclosures.com offers 100 free pre-foreclosure leads — worth testing as a Miami-Dade list source given high pre-foreclosure activity in South Florida
KPI Tracker (flippingmastery.com/kpi) is free and can help the team monitor dials, contacts, leads, and contracts across all four team members
insights.south_florida_relevance: No South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach content was mentioned. However, the pre-foreclosure lead offer (100preforeclosures.com) and absentee owner leads (100absentees.com) are universally applicable and worth testing in the Miami-Dade market. Wholesale regulations by state (wholesaleregulations.com) should be checked for Florida-specific compliance.

videos.title: Tank the Banks.
videos.description:
Most people are playing the game wrong from DAY ONE.

They find a “deal”, run to the bank, get told no, and quit.

The real players?
They don’t even LOOK at deals that need banks.

I’ve got a student, 2 years in, and already quit his job… now buying RV parks AND businesses with creative finance.

He sends me a deal and says:
“Don’t worry only seller financing is on the table.”

That’s the mindset.

You don’t ask:
“Will the bank approve me?”
You ask:
“Will the seller WORK with me?”

Big difference.

And let me make this VERY clear... Not all deals are deals.

If it doesn’t put $15,000+/month NET in your pocket I’m not even considering it.

Once you understand creative finance: You control the terms, create the deal, and stop relying on others approval.

This is how people go from stuck to financially free in a couple years.

Raise your standards. Raise your income.

Head to the main channel and I’ll show you how people are locking up deals without banks right now.

insights.summary: Pace Morby promotes a creative finance mindset that bypasses traditional bank financing by negotiating directly with sellers on terms. The core message is that real investors focus on seller willingness to work a deal rather than bank approval. While not wholesaling-specific, the seller financing and subject-to framework is directly applicable to distressed seller acquisitions.
key_lessons: Shift the qualifying question from 'will the bank approve me?' to 'will the seller work with me?' — this reframes every motivated seller conversation
Not every deal is worth pursuing — set a minimum cash flow or profit threshold and walk away from anything below it (Pace uses $15K/month net as his floor)
Seller financing as a primary exit strategy removes bank dependency and gives you control over terms, timeline, and deal structure
Creative finance (seller financing, sub-to) can be layered onto distressed seller deals as an alternative to straight assignments when ARV margins are thin
Students going from stuck to financially free in 2 years reinforces the urgency of building creative finance skills alongside traditional wholesale assignments
trends: Seller financing is gaining mainstream traction as high interest rates make bank financing unattractive for both buyers and sellers
Investors are expanding beyond single-family into RV parks and businesses using creative finance — signals a maturing market pushing wholesalers to evolve
Anti-bank sentiment is growing among real estate educators, pointing to a broader shift toward terms-based deal structuring
acquisition_one_liners: I'm not going through a bank on this — I work directly with sellers. So my question isn't what a lender will approve, it's what works for YOU.
Would you be open to us structuring this directly between you and me, where you essentially become the bank?
I'm not here to lowball you — I want to know if we can work out terms that get you what you need without waiting on a traditional closing.
A lot of sellers I talk to are more flexible on price when they're in control of the terms. Is that something you'd be open to discussing?
What if we could close this without you having to deal with banks, inspections, or any of that — just straight between us?
disposition_one_liners: This one has seller financing on the table — no bank needed, so your close timeline is clean and terms are negotiable.
I've got a deal locked up with creative terms already in place — if you're looking for a buy-and-hold with built-in financing, let's talk.
The seller is open to carrying the note, which means we can structure this for a cash flow buyer without conventional lending headaches.
tips_and_tricks: Train your acquisition reps to ask 'would the seller consider owner financing?' on every distressed call — even if you plan to wholesale it, knowing seller flexibility opens more exit options
Use the mindset reframe as a tonality tool on calls: position yourself as someone who doesn't need bank approval, which signals confidence and competence to sellers
Set a minimum deal standard internally — don't chase every lead; filter aggressively so the team focuses energy on deals above your profit threshold
When a traditional wholesale margin is too thin, pitch seller financing as an alternative exit to lock up the deal and JV with a creative finance buyer
No transcript was available — this was a short-form description only; for deeper tactical content, direct the team to Pace Morby's main YouTube channel for full creative finance breakdowns
insights.south_florida_relevance: No direct mention of Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, or Florida markets. However, South Florida's high price points and tight margins make seller financing and creative structures increasingly relevant — motivated sellers with equity but needing flexible exits are common in the distressed segment Buying Hero targets.

videos.title: How Virtual Wholesalers Can Beat Local Competition
videos.description:
One of the best virtual wholesaling tips is to keep an in-person touch. 

Having someone visit the property, review photos, and estimate rehab can build trust with sellers and help real estate investors lock up more remote deals. 

That local presence can be the difference between losing a deal and closing it.

#virtualwholesaling #wholesalerealestate #realestateinvesting

insights.summary: This short-form video emphasizes that virtual wholesalers can compete with local operators by maintaining an in-person presence component — having a local contact visit properties, take photos, and estimate rehab costs. The core insight is that a physical touchpoint builds seller trust and helps lock up contracts remotely. Limited content is available as only the video description was provided.
key_lessons: Virtual wholesalers should deploy a local 'boots on the ground' contact (bird dog, driving-for-dollars rep, or local contractor) to visit properties and build seller trust
In-person property visits, photos, and rehab walkthroughs can be the deciding factor in winning a deal over fully remote competitors
Local presence is a competitive differentiator even if the wholesaling operation is run virtually
trends: Virtual wholesaling is competitive enough that local presence has become a key differentiator
Hybrid remote/local operations are emerging as a best practice in wholesaling
acquisition_one_liners: I want to make sure we treat your home with respect — I'm going to send someone out to walk the property personally so we can give you the most accurate offer possible.
Unlike a lot of buyers, we actually have someone local who will come out, take a look, and make sure our offer reflects exactly what your home is worth to us.
I don't want to just throw a number at you over the phone — can I send my local rep out to walk the property so we can put something serious together for you?
disposition_one_liners: We've already had boots on the ground at the property — photos, condition report, and a rough rehab estimate are ready for you to review.
Our local contact walked the property and the numbers make sense — I can send you the full breakdown before you commit to anything.
tips_and_tricks: Build a local network of boots-on-the-ground contacts (bird dogs, contractors, or part-time reps) who can do property walkthroughs on your behalf
Offer sellers an in-person visit as part of your pitch — it differentiates you from purely virtual buyers and builds credibility
Use local contacts to capture photos and basic condition reports to both validate your ARV/rehab estimate and provide buyers with due diligence materials
insights.south_florida_relevance: No specific mention of South Florida, Miami-Dade, Broward, or Palm Beach. However, this tactic is directly applicable to Buying Hero's market — if the team is running remote campaigns or has sellers in outer Miami-Dade or neighboring counties, deploying a local rep or contractor for walkthroughs could increase contract conversion rates against out-of-state virtual competitors targeting the same market.

daily_themes.theme: Gradual increase in aging-seller and estate-driven inventory over the next 10-20 years creates a growing motivated seller pipeline
Slow home price appreciation and affordability pressure may soften ARVs, requiring tighter acquisition underwriting
Inherited property transactions expected to rise as boomer wealth transfers to millennial heirs who may prefer to liquidate
Activity-based KPI tracking replacing revenue-based goals in wholesaling operations
Government list cold calling as a primary distressed seller acquisition channel
High-volume dialing strategies becoming the standard for competitive wholesale markets

top_lessons: Boomers own 41% of all U.S. property and average age 72 — targeting this demographic for cold calls and direct mail is a high-probability long-term strategy
Inventory will increase slowly over 5-20 years, meaning deal flow from aging sellers will grow but won't crater ARVs — good for both acquisitions and disposition pricing
Inherited properties (heirs who receive boomer real estate) are a prime distressed seller segment — heirs often want to liquidate quickly
Home price appreciation is expected to be slow, so locking in assignment fees now before further affordability pressure matters less than volume and consistency
Millennial buyer demand remains strong, supporting end-buyer pools for disposed deals even as inventory rises
3,000 calls from government lists = 1 wholesaling deal (use this as your baseline conversion benchmark)
Set 36,000 annual calls as your north star metric instead of a dollar income goal
Government lists (probate, tax delinquent, code violations, etc.) are the recommended lead source for this call volume strategy
